text.title: azure-nsg-rules Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for azure-nsg-rules as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLMs generate NSG rules based on legacy "Allow 80, 443" examples and forget HTTP/3 (UDP/443 / QUIC). For Front Door and App Gateway frontends, UDP/443 must also be allowed.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Front Door and modern clients negotiate QUIC (HTTP/3) over UDP/443. An NSG that only allows TCP/443 silently falls back to TCP but breaks QUIC performance.

failure-mode.description: Agents drop `network_security_group_id` reference inside the subnet block in Terraform instead of using a separate `azurerm_subnet_network_security_group_association` resource.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inline NSG associations in the subnet block and separate association resources conflict. On plan re-apply the NSG association flaps — intermittent connectivity failures with no error message.

failure-mode.description: Agents propose Service Tags (Storage, Sql) for tight allow-rules then forget those tags are regional; cross-region traffic is not covered by the regional tag.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The `Storage` service tag covers Storage endpoints in the same region. Traffic to a Storage account in another region is not matched and is dropped by the DenyAll rule.
